The component layer handles UI concerns including multi-language text (m_multiBtn_Description Label, m_Txt_Extra_Bet Sprite), button states (on/off/tip), and bar extension animations with specific delay times (EXTRABET_LOGO_OPEN: 0.2s, EXTRABET_LOGO_CLOSE: 0.17s). <observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>MagicPot Server-Client Level Mapping System</title>
  <subtitle>MagicPot maps server levels 0-6 to client display levels 0-3 using floor division to compress state space</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Server levels 0-6 map to client levels 0-3 via formula: Math.floor(serverLevel / 2), with special case 6→3</fact>
    <fact>Mapping is 0,1→0 | 2,3→1 | 4,5→2 | 6→3 for BINGO_LEVEL display compression</fact>
    <fact>SetLevel accepts server level (0-6) and internally calls ConvertServerLevelToClient before SetClientLevelInternal</fact>
    <fact>GetLevel returns m_serverLevel to maintain external contract, preventing double-conversion if caller feeds return value back</fact>
    <fact>Comment BC-9 notes that same-client-level transitions (e.g. server 0→1 both map to client 0) skip LVUP animation</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>MagicPotController implements a critical server-client level mapping to compress seven server states (0-6) into four display states (0-3) for client UI. The ConvertServerLevelToClient method is explicitly non-idempotent—calling it on client values produces incorrect results (0,0,1,1), so the code enforces a strict contract: SetLevel receives server values and performs internal mapping, while SetClientLevelInternal is private for already-mapped values. The m_serverLevel field preserves original server state so GetLevel can return it unchanged, preventing accidental double-conversion bugs. The mapping also affects animation: transitions that don't change the mapped client level (like server 0→1) deliberately skip level-up animations to avoid visual noise.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Collect Symbol Audio and Position Tracking System</title>
  <subtitle>ScatterShowState tracks Collect spine positions and manages tiered Cash SFX levels with one-time CollectBingo loop</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>m_collectSpinePositions array tracks {col, row} positions of all Collect/Multiplier/Cash/MysteryCash spine symbols</fact>
    <fact>m_collectCashSfxLevels Map stores per-position Cash sound effect tiers (1-15) keyed by "col,row" string</fact>
    <fact>m_hasPlayedCollectBingoSfx boolean ensures CollectBingo plays only once per plate collection cycle</fact>
    <fact>m_collectBingoLoopSfxId stores looping SFX ID during eye collection, stopped via StopCollectBingoLoopSfx when complete</fact>
    <fact>All tracking structures reset to empty/default at state entry before PlayCollectSymbolShow execution</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The ScatterShowState implements a sophisticated tracking system for Collect-type symbols during collection sequences. Position tracking (m_collectSpinePositions) records all spine instances created for Collect, Multiplier, Cash, and MysteryCash symbols, enabling coordinated animation and cleanup. The audio system uses two layers: per-symbol Cash SFX with 15-tier progression (m_collectCashSfxLevels keyed by position string) for granular feedback, and a single looping CollectBingo background track (m_collectBingoLoopSfxId) that plays throughout eye collection and stops when the sequence completes. The one-time flag (m_hasPlayedCollectBingoSfx) prevents duplicate CollectBingo triggers within the same plate cycle. All state resets before each new collection run to ensure clean slate behavior.</narrative>
